details: Section 4.2.3 confirms 6-Acetyloxyquinolin-2(1H)-one (2c) m.p. = 221-222 °C.
Experimental section lists 2-[9-(9-Hydroxyfluorenyl)]-2-methylpropanoic acid; M w = 268.31; Melting point: 138 °C.
Table 1 row 3i lists 9,9-dichlorobicyclo[6.1.0]-4-nonylene with bp 110-112/267 Pa, confirmed in characterization section.
Compound 3 in Section 3.1 confirms 2-(8-Hydroxyquinolin-5-yl)-4-nitroisoindoline-1,3-dione m.p. 281-284 °C.
Table 2 lists [Glu(OiPr)2][HCl] T m = 45.72 °C (DSC measurement).
Table 2 confirms 1-Propanol experimental bp = 97.3 °C in the QSPR 100-compound test set.
Compound 13b in experimental section: Piperidine-1-carboxylic acid (5-benzyl-4-phenyl-4H-[1,2,4]triazol-3-thiomethyl)-amide, mp 109 °C.
Table 5 shows Thalidomide SIRM T_b = 650.895 K (calculated by SIRM model); conversion 650.895 - 273.15 = 377.745 °C correct.
The 287-compound QSPR dataset SI Table is not present in the corpus folder (only main PMC article files supplied); anthracene/218.0 quote cannot be located in available paper text.
Compound 24 experimental: '10H-quino[2,3,4-kl]acridin-10-one ... amorphous powder ... m.p. 254°C'.
Compound 3b confirmed: 8-(1-(3-(5'-(Methylthio)-2,2'-bithiophen-5-ylthio)propyl)-1H-1,2,3-triazol-4-yl)octane-1-thiol, mp 74-76 °C.
Table 5 confirms meso-Erythritol DSC onset 118.7 ± 0.21 °C.
Table 2 (Brown 2000) lists toluene mp = 178.2 K; conversion 178.2 - 273.15 = -94.95 °C is correct.
Compound 2j: 5-Acetyl-2-ethyl-4-(2-fluorophenylamino)-6-phenylpyridazin-3(2H)-one, mp = 184-186 °C (EtOH).
Compound 6a: (E,E)-4-Methylbenzal[(2,6-diphenyl-3,5-4H-pyran-4-one-diyl)bis(methylene)]dioxime, m.p. 128°C.
Table 2 confirms 2-Pentanone experimental bp = 102.4 °C in the QSPR 100-compound test set.

Value 247-250 °C and identity of compound 3a are confirmed in paper, but the CSV name has unbalanced parentheses '5-(6'-Thiophen-2''-yl)pyridazin-3'-yl)thiophene-2-carbaldehyde' (missing opening '(' before 'Thiophen' — paper's name pattern is '5-(6'-(Thiophen-2''-yl)pyridazin-3'-yl)thiophene-2-carbaldehyde').
Compound 2c: 2-(Azetidin-1-yl)-N-(2-(3-methoxyphenoxy)phenyl)acetamide, white crystals, m.p. 91-94 °C (iPrOH).

Compound 9 confirmed: (6aR,10aR)-6,6,9-Trimethyl-4-[2-(4-nitrophenyl)hydrazineylidene]-3-pentyl-4,6,6a,7,8,10a-hexahydro-1H-benzo[c]chromen-1-one, mp 76-79 °C. Paper's full name carries a leading (Z)- geometric descriptor not in CSV name, but the (6aR,10aR) absolute stereo and compound number (9) uniquely identify the compound.
Table 2 'Piroxicam-Urea' row, 'Melting point coformer' column = 132-135 — this is the urea coformer's pure mp (urea reference value ~133 °C), correctly attributed to Urea.
Compound 1b: 4-(4-Bromophenyl)-2-Mercapto-6-Oxo-1,6-Dihydropyrimidine-5-Carbonitrile, white powder 85%, mp 284 °C.
Compound 3: (3aS,5R,7aR)-7a-methyl-5-(5-methyl-3-(p-tolyl)-4,5-dihydroisoxazol-5-yl)-3-(p-tolyl)-3a,4,5,6-tetrahydrobenzo[d]isoxazol-7(7aH)-one, mp = 112±2 °C (Ethanol).

Compound 3c: 7-(4-Chlorophenyl)-9-phenyl-7H-tetrazolo[1,5-c]-7H-pyrrolo[3,2-e]pyrimidine, mp 218-20°C (paper). Range correctly expanded to 218-220. CSV omits a middle '-7H-' descriptor that paper includes, but compound identity remains unambiguous.
Compound 3h: 7-(3-Chloro-4-fluorophenyl)-9-(4-methoxyphenyl)tetrazolo[1,5-c]-7H-pyrrolo[3,2-e]pyrimidine, mp 219-21°C; range expansion to 219-221 correct.
Table 2 confirms 1,2-Dichlorobenzene experimental bp = 180.5 °C in the QSPR 100-compound test set.
